<!-- AUTOGENERATED by scripts/gen-readmes.sh — edit scenarios.tsv -->
# Apache HTTPD (HTTP) — root-to-custom-context-servlet-mapping

Proxy at /ui/, app on /app context with VAADIN_URL_MAPPING=/ui/*. The public URL drops the /app prefix; the proxy prepends /app when forwarding to the backend. /ui/HILLA and /ui/connect are rewritten to /app/HILLA and /app/connect (Hilla resources live at the context root, not under the Vaadin servlet mapping). The catch-all carries root-level static resources.

Proxy URL on `http://localhost:9090`: `/ui/`

## Configuration

See [docker-compose.yml](./docker-compose.yml) for service definitions.

Apache HTTPD config (`vaadin.conf`):

```apache
# App on /app context, Vaadin servlet at /ui/*. The public URL drops the /app
# prefix; the proxy prepends /app when forwarding so root-level resources
# (/VAADIN/*, /sw.js, /styles.css, /manifest.webmanifest, /test-redirect, ...)
# and Vaadin servlet paths (/ui/*, including /ui/VAADIN/push) all reach
# /app/<same> on the backend through the catch-all (upgrade=websocket).
#
# Hilla resources live at the backend's context root (/app/HILLA/*,
# /app/connect/*) regardless of vaadin.url-mapping. The browser still calls
# them under the Vaadin mapping (/ui/HILLA/*), so the proxy strips /ui and
# prepends /app for those paths. See https://github.com/vaadin/hilla/issues/289

ProxyPassMatch   "^/ui/(HILLA|connect)/(.*)$"  http://vaadin:8080/app/$1/$2 upgrade=websocket
ProxyPass        "/"                            http://vaadin:8080/app/ upgrade=websocket
ProxyPassReverse "/ui/HILLA/"                   http://vaadin:8080/app/HILLA/
ProxyPassReverse "/ui/connect/"                 http://vaadin:8080/app/connect/
ProxyPassReverse "/"                            http://vaadin:8080/app/
ProxyPassReverseCookiePath "/app" "/"
```
